title: The Environment of a UNIX Process
description: A hierarchical diagram showing the components that constitute the execution environment of a UNIX process, including how they are inherited and managed.
elements: Process Memory Layout (Text, Data, Stack, Heap)
relationships: The kernel creates and manages a PCB for each process, which points to the process's memory layout and other environmental elements. The 'main' function receives pointers to argv[] and envp[]. File descriptors are inherited from the parent process and managed in the kernel's file table. Credentials and signal handlers are stored in the PCB and kernel space.
